excerpt: @Hartmut_Dunker , thanks for your detailed report regarding the BSEED Zigbee 4 Gang Button Switch (TS0726/_TZ3002_pzao9ls1). I see that the device is currently matched to the switch_4gang driver, which is why youre experiencing the issue where all four physical buttons toggle together. Your suggestion to use the wall_switch_4gang_1way driver is insightful, as it appears to have a more appropriate configuration for your device. I will look into this further and see if we can implement the necessary changes to improve the functionality. In the meantime, I recommend checking if there are any firmware updates available for your device, as sometimes manufacturers release updates that can resolve such issues. Additionally, if you havent already, please try removing and re-pairing the device after selecting the correct driver type to see if that helps. On another note, I wanted to share some exciting news from our recent scans. Weve identified several new devices in the Zigbee2MQTT ecosystem, including _TZE200_jt50ea5d , _TZ3210_jaap6jeb , and _TZE284_g1enhdsi . These devices are on our radar for future integration, so stay tuned for updates! Also, there are several open PRs that might interest you, including one for a similar button device that could enhance our support for multi-gang switches. Specifically, PR #1065 aims to improve functionality for the fingerbot, which might provide insights into better handling of button presses. If you continue to experience issues with your device, please let me know, and I can assist further. Your diagnostic report ID (9803c61a-aa73-4e3c-99ed-30a5d0c43973) will also help us track down the problem more effectively. Looking forward to resolving this for you!
